title: Reading and Use of English - Part 6
description: You are going to read four extracts from internet articles about technology in sports. For questions 37 – 40, choose from the experts A – D. The expert may be chosen more than once.

content.title: The Price of the Passport: Mass Tourism

texts.A: The democratization of travel is one of the great achievements of the last century, but we are now reaching a breaking point. [37]While some argue that tourism is a vital lifeline for developing economies, I believe that the 'Instagrammification' of travel has turned cultural heritage into a mere backdrop for selfies, hollowed out of any real meaning.[/37] [39]Furthermore, the environmental cost of budget aviation is a price that future generations will have to pay for our current obsession with weekend getaways.[/39] We are loving our planet to death, and unless we implement strict quotas on visitors to vulnerable sites, we will lose the very beauty we seek to enjoy.
texts.B: It is easy for the wealthy elite to criticize mass tourism, but for many global communities, the influx of travelers is the only thing preventing total economic collapse. [40]Rather than restricting access, we should be using technology to manage flow—smart apps that direct tourists away from overcrowded 'hotspots' toward lesser-known gems.[/40] [37]I share the view that tourism, when managed correctly, acts as a powerful tool for cross-cultural understanding, breaking down the xenophobic barriers that thrive in isolation.[/37] The solution is not to stop traveling, but to travel more intelligently and with a greater sense of local investment.
texts.C: The current debate over 'overtourism' often overlooks the responsibility of the travelers themselves. We have developed a consumerist relationship with the world, where countries are products to be 'done.' [38]Unlike those who believe that better management can fix the industry, I am convinced that the sheer volume of global travel is fundamentally incompatible with ecological preservation.[/38] [39]I am less certain than some of my colleagues that aviation technology will ever be 'green' enough to offset the damage of billions of flights.[/39] We need to rediscover the value of the 'staycation' and realize that the right to travel does not supersede the right of a local community to a quiet, affordable life.
texts.D: The backlash against tourism in European cities is a sign of a deeper systemic failure. When a city's housing market is taken over by short-term rentals, the local culture that tourists came to see evaporates. [38]However, I disagree with the pessimistic view that tourism is inherently destructive; it has provided the funds to restore countless historical sites that would otherwise have fallen into ruin.[/38] [40]I am skeptical, however, that digital solutions like 'crowd-management apps' can solve what is essentially a physical problem of space.[/40] You cannot 'optimize' a medieval square to fit ten thousand people. The only answer is to raise the cost of travel to reflect its true environmental and social impact.

questions.question: Which expert has a different view from B on the social and cultural impact of tourism on local communities?
questions.explanation: B believes tourism promotes 'cross-cultural understanding' and is a lifeline. A, however, argues that it turns culture into a 'hollowed out' backdrop and is damaging to the meaning of heritage.

questions.question: Which expert expresses a different opinion to C regarding whether tourism can be a positive force for preservation?
questions.explanation: C is 'convinced' that travel is 'fundamentally incompatible with ecological preservation.' D disagrees, noting that tourism has 'provided the funds to restore countless historical sites.'

questions.question: Which expert shares C's skepticism about the possibility of environmentally sustainable aviation?
questions.explanation: C is 'less certain' that aviation will ever be green enough. A also highlights the 'environmental cost of budget aviation' as a burden for future generations, implying a similar lack of faith in a technological fix.

questions.question: Which expert has a different opinion to B on the effectiveness of using digital tools to solve overcrowding?
questions.explanation: B advocates for 'smart apps' to manage tourist flow. D is 'skeptical' that such digital solutions can solve the 'physical problem of space' in places like medieval squares.
